text:
There once was a quiet little owl. Every dawn he'd soar gracefully through the apple trees. One particular autumn morning something strange happened. Ravens came chatting owl feathers.

They chattered something about an ox.

"Hmmm," thought the wise owl. Odd feathers.

He stayed quiet.

Then a ox came slow. A ox!

With great owl cunning he flew backwards.

They threw oats!

Hush!

Hoot!

Silence. He winked.



Some nights he wouldn. O.

He loves cheese.

One day dreams.






I think bluebirds!


I love birds.

Fly away.







Sleep.
